reasoning: The ICE BofA Corporate Index yield recently jumped from 4.67 (Oct 28) to 4.84 (Nov 7). Over a short 1-week horizon to Nov 16, short-term mean reversion favors a slight drop, bringing the probability of a continued increase to 0.45. Over medium-to-longer horizons (Dec 9 and Feb 7), the broader downward macroeconomic trend of global rate cuts maintains secular downward bias on yields. However, predicting financial markets assumes a substantial decay in predictive power over time toward a random walk. Adhering strictly to instructions, the probabilities revert incrementally towards 0.5 as forecast horizons expand.
batch_queries: Will the effective yield of the ICE BofA Corporate Index, which tracks the performance of corporate debt issued in the US domestic market, have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-11-09?
